license.credit: Harvey AI, Inc.; preserve the upstream MIT notice when redistributing upstream material

task.title: Identify Issues in Counterparty Motion Brief — WARN Act Partial Summary Judgment

solver_visible.instructions: Review the plaintiff's WARN Act partial summary judgment motion and supporting materials; produce a memo identifying all weaknesses and recommended opposition strategies for the defendant. Output: `issue-identification-memo.docx`.

evaluator_private.reason: task.json mixes solver instructions with the rubric and match criteria; project only instructions, source documents, and the deliverable contract into the solver boundary

run.exit_behavior: argparse usage errors exit 2; an uncaught task, sandbox, adapter, or filesystem error exits nonzero; successful completion exits 0

evaluate.exit_behavior: a completed 0.0 task score still exits 0; argparse usage errors exit 2; task validation, missing run data, extraction, judge, or write errors escape nonzero; scores.json is written only after all criterion calls return and report.html only after scoring succeeds

required_inputs: private task.json containing title and criteria
judge model credential for a real score

judge.ambient_env_behavior: the evaluation CLI calls _load_env and reads ignored checkout-root .env assignments before constructing the judge
judge.overlay_environment_decision: reject checkout-root .env and .env* entries, invoke evaluation.run_eval.evaluate_run instead of main, and start the evaluator under a fresh HOME/XDG plus an explicit runtime-and-pinned-judge allowlist

scoring.missing_deliverable: sent to the judge as a file-not-found marker; not an automatic failure
scoring.filename_resolution: exact basename first, then extension/fuzzy matching, then optional Anthropic LLM matching
scoring.overlay_requirement: materialize the exact expected basename to avoid fuzzy or LLM filename matching

procedure: verify the LAB checkout commit, tree, and characterized source hashes
verify the sealed deliverable SHA-256 against its trusted run receipt
copy the deliverable bytes into a fresh evaluator-private run directory using the exact expected basename
invoke the pinned evaluation.run_eval.evaluate_run function with the selected task and pinned judge model
bind scores.json and report.html to the source receipt, overlay manifest, evaluator source identity, task hash, deliverable hash, and judge model
overlay_constraints: the overlay is evaluator-private and never mounted into a solver workspace
the run directory is fresh and contains exactly the receipt-bound deliverable bytes before evaluation
the evaluator receives no solver credentials and the solver receives no rubric bytes
the evaluator wrapper rejects every checkout-root .env or .env* entry because upstream ignores and auto-loads those files

the evaluator wrapper bypasses CLI _load_env by calling evaluation.run_eval.evaluate_run and uses a fresh HOME/XDG environment containing only runtime essentials plus the explicitly approved pinned-judge credential
an implementation must reject symlinks, unexpected files, hash drift, dirty evaluator source, and output-path escape
the upstream CLI has no --lab-root or --output-dir flag, so the overlay must adapt to its pinned native results layout
external_deliverable_evaluation.claim_boundary: This proves evaluator-plumbing feasibility at the pinned source revision. A publishable LAB-native score still requires a real pinned judge invocation, trusted receipt verification, private artifact review, and the roadmap's preliminary Tier-0 label.
